skill.name: quality-documentation-manager
skill.description: Guides medical device teams through document control, change management, regulatory documentation review, and document management system planning.
skill.summary: Plan and review compliant quality documentation systems for regulated medical device work.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography, Windows SAM, network reconnaissance, and shell backtick patterns. Manual review found these are false positives from compliance terminology, markdown code fences, inline filenames, and placeholder text; no command execution, network access, sensitive file access, prompt injection, or exfiltration evidence was found.

content.value_statement: Regulated teams often struggle to keep quality documents controlled, current, and audit ready. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan document control systems, change workflows, and regulatory documentation reviews.
actual_capabilities: Designs document control frameworks aligned with ISO 13485 expectations.
Outlines regulatory documentation structures for EU MDR, FDA, and international submissions.
Builds change control workflows for document updates, approvals, verification, and closure.
Plans document management system requirements, validation, deployment, and user training.
Defines quality metrics, audit approaches, and continuous improvement actions for document control.
Supports multilingual documentation planning and cross-functional documentation coordination.
limitations: Provides guidance and review structure, not formal regulatory or legal approval.
Needs product details, procedures, jurisdictions, and quality system context for precise recommendations.
Included reference, asset, and script files are placeholders rather than complete operating tools.
Does not connect to a live document management system or validate records automatically.

use_cases.title: Prepare for a Quality System Audit
use_cases.description: Map document control procedures, records, metrics, and training evidence before an ISO 13485 or internal audit.

use_cases.title: Plan a DMS Rollout
use_cases.description: Define document storage, workflow, access control, validation, and deployment needs for an electronic document management system.

use_cases.title: Review Submission Documentation
use_cases.description: Check regulatory documentation packages for structure, traceability, completeness, and jurisdiction-specific expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Document Control Gaps
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an existing process
prompt_templates.prompt: Review our current document control process for ISO 13485 gaps. Focus on approval, version control, distribution, retention, and obsolete document handling.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Change Control Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a document change control workflow for a medical device quality system. Include request intake, impact assessment, approval, implementation, training, verification, and closure.

prompt_templates.title: Plan DMS Requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: Create requirements for an electronic document management system supporting 21 CFR Part 11, ISO 13485, controlled distribution, audit trails, and role-based access.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Regulatory Documentation Review Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Develop a review plan for a medical device regulatory documentation package. Cover technical documentation, risk management, clinical evidence, manufacturing records, traceability, and approval criteria.

output_examples.input: We need to improve document control before an ISO 13485 audit.
output: A gap assessment organized by document lifecycle stage.
A prioritized action list for approval workflows, revision control, distribution, and retention.
Audit evidence recommendations for procedures, records, training, and metrics.

output_examples.input: We are choosing a new electronic document management system.
output: A requirements checklist for access control, audit trails, electronic signatures, workflow routing, search, backup, and validation.
A phased implementation plan covering configuration, testing, training, migration, and release readiness.

output_examples.input: We need a review plan for EU MDR technical documentation.
output: A review structure covering Annex II and Annex III documentation areas.
A traceability approach linking design, risk, clinical, manufacturing, and post-market documents.
Decision criteria for readiness, open issues, and escalation.

best_practices: Provide the applicable regulations, product type, document list, and current procedure before asking for a review.
Ask for traceability checks across requirements, risk controls, verification, validation, and regulatory submissions.
Use the output as a structured draft and route final decisions through qualified quality and regulatory reviewers.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated guidance as final regulatory approval.
Do not request generic templates without explaining your document lifecycle, roles, and approval rules.
Do not skip human review for submissions, audits, validation protocols, or controlled procedures.

faq.question: What type of documentation does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports quality system documents, regulatory documentation, change records, DMS planning materials, audit evidence, and training documentation.

faq.question: Can it replace a quality or regulatory expert?
faq.answer: No. It provides structure and review support, but qualified personnel must approve regulated decisions and controlled documents.

faq.question: Does it create complete document templates?
faq.answer: It can draft outlines and content structures. The included asset and reference files are placeholders, so finished templates require user input.

faq.question: Which standards does it reference?
faq.answer: The skill discusses ISO 13485, ISO 14971, IEC 62304, EU MDR, FDA submission documentation, and 21 CFR Part 11 concepts.

faq.question: Can it work with existing company procedures?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the procedure text, document list, roles, and jurisdictional requirements for a focused review.

faq.question: Does it access external systems?
faq.answer: No evidence of external system access was found. It is written as documentation guidance and includes only a placeholder script.
